meaning: n.[心]性受虐狂,受虐色情狂,受虐狂
examples: You spent the whole weekend in a tent in the rain? That's masochism!
Psychoanalysts tend to regard both sadism and masochism as arising from childhood deprivation.
Once you have tasted life in southern California, it takes a peculiar kind of masochism to return to a British winter.
He is doing what Alastair Campbell, Tony Blair's former Director of Communications, used to call the "masochism strategy".
Unsurprisingly, once we phrase tax policy as a collective exercise in fiscal masochism, our threshold for tax pain turns out to be very low.
